title: Peristylus gardneri
taxonomy: Peristylus gardneri is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae, specifically in the subfamily Orchidoideae and the tribe Diurideae. It is a type of terrestrial orchid native to Australia. The genus Peristylus is part of the subtribe Thysaniellinae. The classification is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic analysis. Peristylus gardneri is further categorized within the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Liliopsida, and order Asparagales.

It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, where its roots are valued for their potential health benefits, including their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in treating various ailments such as respiratory issues and fever.
